summary: Closed, unmerged PR that improved `src/melee/lb/lbsnap.c` matching by replacing placeholder-style code and signatures with typed helpers, clearer control flow, and register-allocation-oriented locals. The PR body reports `main/melee/lb/lbsnap` at 20/22 functions 100%, with remaining blockers `lbSnap_8001DA5C` at 55.87963% and `lbSnap_8001DF20` at 98.15790%. The decomp.dev bot reported five new matches and one additional improvement in `lbSnap_8001DA5C`.

key_files.role: Main matching work: added static inline helpers, rewrote repeated text clearing and return flow, refined tiled RGB offset math, adjusted time conversion typing, and reused save-data offset computation.
Header cleanup: changed `lbSnap_8001E27C` from `UNK_RET lbSnap_8001E27C(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void lbSnap_8001E27C(void)`.

what_changed: Added `static inline void lbSnap_ClearText(char* text)` and used it to replace repeated 0x21-byte manual zeroing loops in `lbSnap_8001D5FC`, `lbSnap_8001D7B0`, and `lbSnap_8001E058`.
Changed several card-result wrappers to cache `ptr->card_result` in a local `ret`, run the operation only when `ret == 0`, assign the callee result back to `ret`, and return once.

Reworked `lbSnap_8001DA5C` from register-named temporaries (`r4`, `r5`, `r6`, etc.) into typed and semantically named locals such as `dst`, `dst_x`, `src_x`, `dst_col`, `tile_column`, `dst_y`, `src_y_accum`, and `pixel`.
Added tiled texture address helpers `lbSnap_GetTiledRGBOffset(int x, int y, int tile_stride)` and `lbSnap_GetTiledYOff(int tile_column, int y)` for the RGB565-to-RGB5A3 icon conversion path.
Adjusted `lbSnap_8001DC0C` time conversion by storing `OSTicksToSeconds(ticks)` in a `u32 seconds` and calling `OSTicksToCalendarTime(OSSecondsToTicks((u64) seconds), &time)` inline.
Added `lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset(struct Unk80433380_0* snap)` and reused it in `lbSnap_8001DF20` and `lbSnap_8001DF6C` instead of repeating the offset arithmetic inline.
In `lbSnap_8001DF6C`, introduced local `struct Unk803BACC8* desc = &lbSnap_803BACC8` and `int chan_arg = chan`, removed a `PAD_STACK(8)`, and passed `&desc->x14`/`desc` into `lb_8001BB48`.
Updated the header declaration for `lbSnap_8001E27C` to the concrete signature `void lbSnap_8001E27C(void)`.

Verification in the PR body claimed `python configure.py && ninja` and `main/melee/lb/lbsnap`: 20/22 functions at 100%. The bot report listed five new 100% matches: `lbSnap_8001DF6C`, `lbSnap_8001D7B0`, `lbSnap_8001D5FC`, `lbSnap_8001E058`, and `lbSnap_8001DC0C`.
smart_moves: Factoring the repeated fixed-size text clear into a static inline helper let multiple near-identical functions share the same code shape without changing behavior.
Using a `ret` local and a single return path matched several card-result wrappers better than returning directly from both branches.

Replacing opaque register temporaries in `lbSnap_8001DA5C` with typed pointer math made the tiled texture sampling logic searchable and easier to continue, even though that function remained unmatched.
The tiled offset helpers captured the GameCube-style 4x4 tile addressing formula in one place, reducing repeated arithmetic noise during matching work.
The save-data offset helper made the `xC + (&x38 - base)` pattern explicit and reusable across `lbSnap_8001DF20` and `lbSnap_8001DF6C`.
The `OSTicksToSeconds`/`OSSecondsToTicks` type adjustment in `lbSnap_8001DC0C` was small but enough for the bot to report that function moving from 99.97% to 100%.
Introducing local aliases like `desc` and `chan_arg` in `lbSnap_8001DF6C` is a typical ralloc-sensitive tactic for matching MWCC output.
decomp_lessons: For repeated stack-buffer clearing, try a `static inline` helper before forcing each function to carry its own hand-written loop.
When a wrapper checks a status field and either returns it or calls another function, caching the status in `ret` and using one final return can be important for matching.
Prefer typed base pointers for offset expressions: `snap->xC + ((int) &snap->x38 - (int) snap)` is clearer and more reusable than ad hoc references through nested globals.
Small integer type choices matter in OS time code; assigning seconds to `u32` and casting back to `u64` at the call site affected matching in `lbSnap_8001DC0C`.
For tiled texture copies, extract the tile-stride and intra-tile address math into helpers so later work can focus on loop structure and register allocation.
Do not assume cleanup equals a full match: `lbSnap_8001DA5C` became much clearer but was still only reported at 55.87963%, and `lbSnap_8001DF20` still had a register-allocation-only blocker.
When the implementation signature is known, replace `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` header placeholders with explicit prototypes to reduce future ambiguity.
naming_conventions: `lbSnap_*` functions use the library prefix plus address-style suffix, e.g. `lbSnap_8001D5FC`, `lbSnap_8001DA5C`, and `lbSnap_8001DF20`.
New internal helpers used `lbSnap_` plus PascalCase descriptive names: `lbSnap_ClearText`, `lbSnap_GetTiledRGBOffset`, `lbSnap_GetTiledYOff`, and `lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset`.
Unknown structs retained address/offset-style names such as `Unk80433380_48`, `Unk80433380_0`, and `Unk803BACC8`.
Partially understood fields kept `xNN` offset notation while adding semantic suffixes where available, e.g. `x54_stateChanged`, `x44_LbMcSnap_MemSnapIconData`, and `x4_string`.
The diff moved away from raw register placeholder names in `lbSnap_8001DA5C` toward role names: `dst_x`, `src_x`, `dst_col`, `tile_column`, `dst_y`, `src_y_accum`, and `pixel`.
Known status fields used direct semantic names such as `card_result`, `num`, `entries`, and `time`.
Header declarations should prefer explicit signatures like `void lbSnap_8001E27C(void)` once known, instead of `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Static inline helpers were used to preserve reusable codegen patterns while simplifying source.
Several functions switched from branch-local direct returns to `ret` assignment plus one final `return ret;`.

`PAD_STACK` usage was adjusted: the new helper-based code kept `PAD_STACK(8)` in several stack-buffer functions, reduced `lbSnap_8001DA5C` from `PAD_STACK(16)` to `PAD_STACK(8)`, and removed `PAD_STACK(8)` from `lbSnap_8001DF6C`.
Pointer aliases such as `struct Unk803BACC8* desc` and local copies such as `int chan_arg = chan` were introduced, likely to influence register allocation in matched functions.
The RGB path used explicit `u8*` and `u16*` casts plus address-of indexed byte arrays: `*(u16*) &((u8*) arg0)[offset]` and `*(u16*) &dst_col[offset]`.
The RGB565-to-RGB5A3 conversion macro remained central, while the surrounding tiled address math was factored into `lbSnap_GetTiledRGBOffset` and `lbSnap_GetTiledYOff`.

The bot report gives concrete matching evidence: `lbSnap_8001DF6C`, `lbSnap_8001D7B0`, `lbSnap_8001D5FC`, `lbSnap_8001E058`, and `lbSnap_8001DC0C` reached 100%; `lbSnap_8001DA5C` improved from 47.05% to 55.88%.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
The PR was closed without being merged, so these changes should be treated as useful evidence rather than accepted project history unless found elsewhere.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 67.05%, a +0.06% change and +2424 bytes.
The bot reported five new matches in `main/melee/lb/lbsnap`: `lbSnap_8001DF6C`, `lbSnap_8001D7B0`, `lbSnap_8001D5FC`, `lbSnap_8001E058`, and `lbSnap_8001DC0C`.
The PR body listed remaining blockers as `lbSnap_8001DA5C`: 55.87963% and `lbSnap_8001DF20`: 98.15790% with register allocation only.
follow_up_queries: Search later merged PRs for `lbSnap_8001DA5C` to see whether the tiled RGB/icon-copy loop was finally matched.
Search later merged PRs for `lbSnap_8001DF20` register allocation fixes.
Check whether `lbSnap_ClearText` or the `ret` single-return pattern was reused in accepted `lbsnap` work.
Look for a merged version of the `lbSnap_8001E27C(void)` header prototype change.
Compare current `src/melee/lb/lbsnap.c` against PR #2495 to see which closed-PR tactics were cherry-picked or superseded.
Investigate whether the `lbSnap_GetSaveDataOffset` expression reveals a better name or layout for `Unk80433380_0::xC` and `x38`.
